official.name: Raphael G. Warnock
official.office_address: 717 Hart Senate Office Building Washington DC 20510

summary.narrative: Warnock's strongest documented delivery this period is the enacted $35 insulin cap for Medicare seniors, which he primarily sponsored. On healthcare and housing affordability he sponsored multiple bills (Bridge to Medicaid, Stop Predatory Investing, Downpayment Toward Equity, Rent Relief), most of which remain in committee. His voting-rights and anti-gerrymandering commitments are documented through introduced legislation and consistent public advocacy but have not been enacted, reflecting dependence on a non-aligned Congress.
summary.grade_rationale: The record pairs one fully enacted, high-clarity quantitative win (insulin cap) with several specific, ambitious bills that reached only introduction or committee stage, plus directional commitments tied to broad national legislation outside his unilateral control. The pattern is consistent legislative action and advocacy with limited enactment beyond the insulin measure.

data_gaps: Several promises restated in 2026 official press releases (c25) are treated as continuations of campaign-era commitments per the era-confirmation note; some delivery attributions (Medicare negotiation, ACA) rely on broad reconciliation context rather than Warnock-specific sponsorship and carry LIMITED EVIDENCE. No promise-stated deadlines were available, so all time_pressure values are null. Excluded items (reproductive justice, infrastructure/green jobs, decriminalize marijuana, Equality Act, police accountability) were either value statements, lacked confirmable delivery evidence in the material, or were not reachable with confident bucket assignment; they could be revisited with additional sources.

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 57.8% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
